name: Gold
summary: Day 63 Thursday — GOLD $4,581/oz (-0.2% 1D, +1.2% 1M, -13.9% 3M); DAY 10 OF INFLATION-REGIME CALL — POWELL VERBAL VALIDATION ON THE RECORD ('IT HASN'T EVEN PEAKED YET'). Gold should have spiked 1-2% on Trump-Axios 'choking like a stuffed pig' rejection of Hormuz-First + Brent $118-120 + Iran rial 1.8M + Hormuz Day 62. Instead gold is FLAT-TO-DOWN (-0.2%). Why underperforming (UNCHANGED but REINFORCED): (1) US 10Y at 4.42% (+1.5% 1D / +7bps) — real yields competing harder; Powell's 'energy inflation hasn't even peaked yet' framing means Fed-cut catalyst is FURTHER out (CME FedWatch: 0% Apr cut, 30% by Dec from prior 70% in Mar). (2) DXY at 98.99 (+0.4% 1D, breaks 98.7 ceiling) — dollar firms on hawkish-skewed FOMC dissent (3 wanted MORE hawkish framing vs 1 dovish); EM-currency safe-haven demand absorbed by USD not gold. (3) Crowded positioning persists (COMEX gold longs near multi-year highs). (4) Crisis priced as INFLATION event, not FINANCIAL-SYSTEM-RISK event. POWELL VERBAL VALIDATION OF REGIME ON THE RECORD: yesterday's brief was at Day 9 of empirical confirmation; today is Day 10 with Powell's explicit verbal validation. The graph downgrade of gold $4,600 trigger from active to watching (yesterday) was correct AND should NOW BE MOVED FROM WATCHING TO RESOLVED-INACTIVE until regime shift signal (EM-Asia FX-stress contagion confirmed via Indonesian rupiah record-low Apr 29 / Indian rupee at ₹95.00+ sustained / Gulf bank or sovereign credit-spread widening / US bank with Iran-counterparty disclosure). For investor: DO NOT add to gold above $4,800 in this regime. [TRUNCATED — needs re-synthesis]
